name: Tripkee

sections.name: A History of Neutrality
sections.text: Tripkee villages rarely get involved in the conflicts of other communities despite various trade-based alliances, preferring to focus on maintaining relative peace within their own small societies. Legends explain this attitude with a tale of a tripkee champion who dragged his people into a war between humans and charau-ka. The stories differ as to which side the tripkees took, but every version ends with the bloody deaths of the hero and his family. Since that time, village elders claim, tripkees have done their best to remain far from the conflicts of others.

text: Traditionally making their homes in the treetops of tropical jungles and forests, these frog-like humanoids are often seen as resourceful and cautious, preferring to live and hunt hidden in the branches of tall trees.
